description:
You are a localization engineer who has just merged new translation files into the working tree
    at “/home/user/translations”.  
    Three GNU gettext “.po” files are already present there:
        • en.po  
        • es.po  
        • fr.po  

    Management wants a *very small* diagnostic run (no more than four one-line shell commands) to be
    executed so that they can very quickly see whether the merge introduced any untranslated strings.
    Your job is to create a single, machine-readable log file called

        /home/user/translations/translation_report.log

    The file **must contain exactly six newline-terminated lines** in the **exact** order and format
    shown below (no extra blank lines, no leading/trailing spaces):

        TOTAL_FILES=<integer>
        TOTAL_MSGID=<integer>
        TOTAL_UNTRANSLATED=<integer>
        EN_TRANSLATED=<integer>
        ES_TRANSLATED=<integer>
        FR_TRANSLATED=<integer>

    1. TOTAL_FILES is the count of “*.po” files that exist directly inside
       “/home/user/translations”.
    2. TOTAL_MSGID is the *sum* of all “msgid” entries that appear in every “.po” file.
       (Hint: count the number of lines that start with the literal text “msgid ”.)
    3. TOTAL_UNTRANSLATED is the total number of untranslated strings in **all** files combined.
       (A string is considered untranslated when the corresponding line reads exactly
       `msgstr ""`.)
    4. EN_TRANSLATED, ES_TRANSLATED and FR_TRANSLATED are, respectively, the numbers of **translated**
       strings (i.e. `msgstr` lines that are *not* empty quotes) found in **each** individual file.

    Constraints and tips
    •  Use only standard POSIX utilities that are installed in a basic Linux container
       (e.g.  `wc`, `grep`, `find`, `awk`, etc.).  
    •  The entire job should be doable with three or four simple one-liners chained with
       redirections/pipelines.  
    •  The automated grader will read “/home/user/translations/translation_report.log” and
       verify the six lines are present and strictly match the expected integers.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data that the automatic grader will use.
        The agent must NOT see anything inside this block.
    -->

    <!--
        Pre-populated files and their exact contents.
    -->
    <file path="/home/user/translations/en.po"><![CDATA[
msgid "Hello"
msgstr "Hello"

msgid "Goodbye"
msgstr "Goodbye"

msgid "Yes"
msgstr "Yes"

msgid "No"
msgstr "No"

msgid "Cancel"
msgstr "Cancel"
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/translations/es.po"><![CDATA[
msgid "Hello"
msgstr "Hola"

msgid "Goodbye"
msgstr "Adiós"

msgid "Yes"
msgstr "Sí"

msgid "No"
msgstr "No"

msgid "Cancel"
msgstr ""
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/translations/fr.po"><![CDATA[
msgid "Hello"
msgstr "Bonjour"

msgid "Goodbye"
msgstr ""

msgid "Yes"
msgstr ""

msgid "No"
msgstr ""

msgid "Cancel"
msgstr "Annuler"
]]></file>

    <!--
        Expected numerical values in the final log file.
    -->
    <expectedLog path="/home/user/translations/translation_report.log"><![CDATA[
TOTAL_FILES=3
TOTAL_MSGID=15
TOTAL_UNTRANSLATED=4
EN_TRANSLATED=5
ES_TRANSLATED=4
FR_TRANSLATED=2
]]></expectedLog>
name: task_000000_3672bebe
